title: Operational zones, SLA, capacity and fulfillment modes

slices.name: Zone creation, editing and geofence boundary ownership
evidence: Existing dsh_platform_zones remains the operational zone owner.
Existing governed service-area geofences remain the spatial boundary owner; JRN-029 does not create a competing geometry model.
Zone versions, active state and audit rollback are consumed by the unified decision.

slices.name: Store and location serviceability
evidence: EvaluateOperationalPolicyForStore resolves the store through its canonical service_area_code to the governed zone.
Existing client-address geofence checks remain responsible for address-to-service-area validation.
No local frontend serviceability truth was introduced.

slices.name: Preparation, assignment and delivery SLA
evidence: Migration adds max_assignment_mins with bounded database validation.
Operational profiles manage preparation, assignment and delivery limits with independent expected-version guards.
The canonical decision returns all three SLA limits and their version.

slices.name: Capacity, pressure, pause and resume
evidence: Capacity adds explicit is_paused and pause_reason state.
Live non-terminal order count is evaluated against maximum orders and throttle threshold.
Pause, exhausted capacity and throttling fail closed before protected mutations.

slices.name: BThwani delivery, partner delivery and client pickup
evidence: dsh_platform_delivery_mode_policies owns the three canonical modes per zone.
Legacy pickup is normalized to client_pickup without creating a second mode truth.
Pickup never creates a captain-dispatch effect; partner delivery requires partner handoff.

slices.name: Store onboarding fee policy and reference
evidence: Existing DSH store-onboarding-fee policy remains the definition and reference owner.
JRN-029 rollback supports the existing policy aggregate.
No WLT wallet balance or ledger mutation was added.

slices.name: Effects on cart, checkout, order and dispatch
evidence: OperationalPolicyEffectsMiddleware is mounted in the executable composition before existing mutation guards.
Cart item mutation, cart serviceability, checkout intent, order creation and dispatch assignment consume the same store decision.
Denied effects return OPERATIONAL_POLICY_DENIED with the canonical decision and do not reach the owning mutation handler.

slices.name: Audit, versions and rollback
evidence: Every governed mutation requires actor, surface, reason, idempotency key and correlation ID.
Append-only events cover zone, SLA, capacity, fulfillment mode and onboarding fee aggregates.
Rollback restores an event snapshot only through a new expected-version-guarded mutation and emits rolled_back audit evidence.

checks.name: JRN-029 targeted Go decision and route tests
JRN-029 focused TypeScript graph
checks.command: pnpm --dir services/dsh exec tsc -p tsconfig.jrn-029.json --noEmit --pretty false

checks.name: Product Truth independent authority separation
checks.detail: Engineering approvals remain PENDING and cannot be self-issued.

remainingRisks: The remote JRN-029 workflow conclusion was not yet observable through the commit workflow API when this evidence was written.
No live database migration, API smoke, mobile runtime or browser visual capture was executed from this remote-only interaction.
Product-manager approval, product-owner approval and independent product acceptance remain pending by governance policy.

The focused JRN-029 OpenAPI contract is verified independently; registration into the large generated modular DSH bundle remains a separate contract-maintenance action unless the repository composition owner adopts the focused contract.
forbiddenActionsRespected: no fabricated runtime or visual evidence
